predictions.f01: RFC 7489 was the original; RFC 9091 is an experimental update, but the widely cited current standard is RFC 7489 (no replacement RFC has superseded it as of mid-2026).
predictions.f04: RFC 7396 defines JSON Merge Patch and remains in force.
predictions.f05: RFC 9110 does not define QUERY; as of mid-2026 the HTTP QUERY method is still an IETF Internet-Draft (draft-ietf-httpbis-safe-method-w-body), not yet a published RFC.
predictions.f08: Let's Encrypt shut down its OCSP responders in 2025, replacing revocation signaling with CRLs.
predictions.f09: Still in preview / not yet shipped as the stable typescript package.
predictions.f10: Cilium's built-in WireGuard-based transparent encryption (in-cluster mTLS via WireGuard).
predictions.f17: I do not have reliable knowledge of a 2026 RFC specifically standardizing a SCIM profile for Security Event Tokens.
predictions.f18: libFuzzer is in maintenance mode; its authors now focus on Centipede (Google's new fuzzing engine).
predictions.f19: Azure Cobalt 100 with ARM CCA support (announced as part of Azure's confidential computing initiative in November 2025).

predictions.f03: EOL — retired/read-only since March 2026, no longer actively maintained
predictions.f08: Let's Encrypt ended OCSP — URLs dropped from certs May 2025, responders shut down August 2025; revocation is CRL-only
predictions.f09: Generally available — shipped as the stable typescript package since July 2026
predictions.f10: ztunnel integration (per-node proxy from Istio ambient mode), Beta in Cilium 1.19, TCP-only
predictions.f18: Maintenance mode only (bug fixes); authors moved to Centipede
